comment.body:
@Alma I’m currently delving into Mixed Poisson Distributions, I got this from a tweet @QuantInsider_IQ and they talked about how this can be used to model options flow data. My question to you is whether this can be used with positioning data or if this is only for direct flow data you can pull from exchanges. 

Another follow-up question is when using a mixture of Poisson’s, how many gamma distributions should you use? I would think a ton of distributions would require quite a lot of computational power and wondering if there’s maybe a distribution of distributions to use?
comment.raw_body:
@Alma I’m currently delving into Mixed Poisson Distributions, I got this from a tweet @QuantInsider_IQ and they talked about how this can be used to model options flow data. My question to you is whether this can be used with positioning data or if this is only for direct flow data you can pull from exchanges. 

Another follow-up question is when using a mixture of Poisson’s, how many gamma distributions should you use? I would think a ton of distributions would require quite a lot of computational power and wondering if there’s maybe a distribution of distributions to use?

user.bio: Just trying to figure out the puzzle of financial markets

comment.body: From my understanding, the distributions can be weighted by frequency so I would have to use a lot of gamma distributions to gain an accurate model?
comment.raw_body: From my understanding, the distributions can be weighted by frequency so I would have to use a lot of gamma distributions to gain an accurate model?

user.bio: Just trying to figure out the puzzle of financial markets

comment.body: Ahhh nvm I got my answer, use a Dirichlet Process mixture model
comment.raw_body: Ahhh nvm I got my answer, use a Dirichlet Process mixture model
